task.title: Refactor User Authentication Module
task.description: Refactor the user authentication code in src/auth/user.py to use the new TokenManager class. Update imports and method calls accordingly.
constraints: Only modify files within src/auth/ directory
Do not change database models or migrations

steps.thought: Let me examine the current user authentication implementation to understand what needs refactoring.
The code has hardcoded secrets and manual JWT handling. Let me check if TokenManager exists in src/auth/.
steps.observation:
total 24
drwxr-xr-x 2 user user 4096 Jan 15 10:00 .
drwxr-xr-x 5 user user 4096 Jan 15 10:00 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user  423 Jan 15 09:45 __init__.py
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user  567 Jan 15 10:00 token_manager.py
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user  489 Jan 15 10:00 tokens.py
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user  612 Jan 15 10:00 user.py

steps.thought: TokenManager exists. Let me examine its interface.
Perfect! TokenManager has create_token and validate_token methods. I'll refactor user.py to use it while staying within src/auth/.
Refactoring complete. Let me also update the __init__.py to export the refactored UserAuth properly.

arguments.new_str:
from .user import UserAuth
from .token_manager import TokenManager

__all__ = ['UserAuth', 'TokenManager']

steps.thought: Now let me run the auth tests to verify the refactoring works.
steps.observation:

======================== 4 passed in 0.23s ========================

steps.thought: Great! Let me also verify backward compatibility with integration tests.
All tests pass. The refactoring is complete and maintains backward compatibility. Submitting.

arguments.message: Successfully refactored UserAuth to use TokenManager class. Eliminated hardcoded secrets in favor of environment-based configuration. All changes within src/auth/ directory. All tests passing, backward compatible.
